commit.message:
fix(r2): infinite retry with exponential backoff — uploads never fail

Upload retries indefinitely (1s, 2s, 4s, ... capped at 60s) until
success. R2 failures are always transient network issues — no reason
to give up and fall back to local storage.

commit.message:
fix(r2): make upload failures loud instead of silent

R2 upload() now raises R2UploadError instead of returning None.
Callers catch and log.error with local fallback — no more silent data loss.
Retries increased 3→5, initial delay lowered 2s→1s.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: decouple translations from image loading to prevent timeout

The previous approach ran up to 4 sequential Gemini calls inside
fetchAllImageCache before returning any entries, easily exceeding
Vercel's 10s function timeout and causing no images to load.

- fetchAllImageCache now returns entries immediately (fast Firestore
  read only, no Gemini calls).
- A new translateMissingEntries action handles translations separately.
- The component fires translations as a parallel, non-blocking Promise
  after the images are already visible on screen.
- Added .catch() guards so neither step can crash the component.

commit.message:
fix: CRITICAL — Remove wrong canonical tag that blocked Google indexing

The root layout set canonical: "/" which made EVERY page on the site
tell Google "I'm a duplicate of the homepage." This caused 0 pages
to be indexed for 3 months (confirmed by GSC coverage report).

Fix: Removed the global canonical from root layout metadata.

Also added www → non-www 301 redirect in middleware to consolidate
link equity and prevent duplicate content from www subdomain.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix OmniAuth CSRF session key mismatch (#138)

* Fix OmniAuth CSRF session key mismatch

OmniAuth's built-in AuthenticityTokenProtection uses Rack::Protection's
:csrf session key, but Rails stores its CSRF token under :_csrf_token.
This session key mismatch causes "Forbidden" errors on the admin login
form.

Disable OmniAuth's request_validation_phase since OAuth providers are
already protected by the state parameter in the callback phase.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix NavigationRegistry.reset! to re-register default user menu

reset! clears all class-level sections, including the "My Account"
bottom section registered during Configuration#initialize. When
nested_navigation_spec calls reset! in its after block, the user
menu disappears for any spec that runs later — causing
order-dependent failures in logins_spec (seed 30301).

Re-registering defaults after clearing ensures the user menu is
always present after a reset.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update reset! spec to expect default My Account section

The reset! method now re-registers the default user menu after
clearing, so the unit test must expect ["My Account"] instead
of an empty sections array.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

  returns keys that exactly match the cache key format (e.g. "aimer_fr"),

  Pexels keyword, and use the Portuguese translation as the fallback query
  instead of the raw foreign word.

Fix Dockerfile: simplify build stages and add .dockerignore

missing the public directory. Simplified to a clean two-stage build.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: adaptive sync sweep interval to reduce idle CPU

The sync sweep was running every 1s, calling BatchIterateSessions which
walks all ~16M hash buckets in the 10M-entry BPF session map — even when
there are zero sessions to sync. This caused __htab_map_lookup_and_delete_batch
to consume ~93% of bpfrxd CPU on idle cluster nodes.

Two optimizations:
1. Counter-based fast-path: read GLOBAL_CTR_SESSIONS_NEW/CLOSED (O(1)
   per-CPU counter reads) to skip the O(buckets) BatchIterate when no
   sessions have changed since the last empty sweep.
2. Adaptive interval: back off exponentially (1s → 2s → 4s → 8s → 10s)
   when nothing needs syncing. Return to 1s immediately when sessions
   are synced, keeping replication lag minimal under load.

Verified on loss cluster: bpfrxd CPU dropped from ~15% sustained to 0.0%.

commit.message:
feat: mouse wheel scrolls file panel listing

Handle MouseFlags.WheeledUp/WheeledDown in OnMouseClick. Scrolling over
an inactive panel also activates it first (natural gesture).

ScrollBy(delta) moves _scrollOffset by 3 rows per wheel tick in Full mode,
or by one column at a time in Brief (two-column) mode to keep _scrollOffset
on a column boundary. After scrolling, _cursorIndex is clamped into the
newly visible range so it never goes off-screen.

  zoom inicial 0.5→0.35 para área de trabalho maior visível

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: invoke Properties via ShellExecuteEx instead of IContextMenu::InvokeCommand

The built-in Properties handler fails with "The properties for this item
are not available" when called via a numeric MAKEINTRESOURCE verb from a
non-Explorer process — it cannot resolve the file PIDL path in that context.

Fix: use IContextMenu::GetCommandString(GCS_VERBW) to detect when the user
chose the "properties" verb, then bypass InvokeCommand and call ShellExecuteEx
with lpVerb="properties", lpFile=<path>, and SEE_MASK_INVOKEIDLIST instead.
SEE_MASK_INVOKEIDLIST tells the shell to go through its own PIDL mechanism,
which correctly opens the Properties sheet regardless of the calling process.
All other verbs continue to use InvokeCommand as before.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add Vercel Analytics for visitor tracking

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: revert to GetUIObjectOf + add IContextMenu2/3 owner-draw message forwarding

Two fixes in one:

1. Revert from SHCreateDefaultContextMenu back to IShellFolder::GetUIObjectOf.
   SHCreateDefaultContextMenu introduced the "properties not available" error
   because its pidlFolder + psf combination was inconsistent, causing the
   built-in Properties handler to fail to resolve the item path.

2. Add IContextMenu2 / IContextMenu3 support with a custom WndProc.
   Many shell extensions (7-Zip's submenu icon, others) use owner-drawn menu
   items via WM_INITMENUPOPUP / WM_DRAWITEM / WM_MEASUREITEM. Without
   forwarding these messages to IContextMenu2::HandleMenuMsg the items are
   inserted into the HMENU but render as blank/invisible entries. The custom
   WndProc now forwards all four relevant messages; IContextMenu3 is preferred
   when available (adds HandleMenuMsg2 for WM_MENUCHAR support).

   [ThreadStatic] fields share the current IContextMenu2/3 pointers with the
   static WndProc delegate safely — both always execute on the same STA thread.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: use SHGetIDListFromObject to get pidlFolder instead of re-parsing the path

Re-parsing the parent directory via SHParseDisplayName can produce a
PIDL that differs subtly from what psfRaw actually represents (different
case, junction/symlink resolution, etc.). DEFCONTEXTMENU.pidlFolder +
pidlChild are combined by the Properties handler to resolve the file path;
a mismatch causes "The properties for this item are not available".

Fix: call SHGetIDListFromObject(psfRaw) to obtain the folder's own
canonical PIDL — guaranteed to be consistent with the IShellFolder we
already have, so Properties and other verbs can resolve the item path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: use SHCreateDefaultContextMenu with explicit registry keys to load all extension handlers

Replace IShellFolder::GetUIObjectOf with SHCreateDefaultContextMenu —
the same function Explorer uses internally. The key difference is that
DEFCONTEXTMENU.aKeys lets us pass the registry keys (HKCR\*, HKCR\<ext>,
HKCR\<ProgID>, HKCR\AllFilesystemObjects) that the shell uses to enumerate
dynamic context menu handlers. Without these, only static verbs from the
IShellFolder itself appear (e.g. "Run as administrator" from HKCR\exefile\
shell\runas), while COM-based extension handlers like 7-Zip and Notepad++
(registered under HKCR\*\shellex\ContextMenuHandlers) are skipped.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
